neighbors.name: Darrel D Howell & Lee Ann Howell
neighbors.claims: Darrel D. Howell and Lee Ann Howell are residents of Cochise County, Arizona. Lee Ann Howell is a prominent science teacher at Colonel Smith Middle School in the Fort Huachuca Accommodation School District ([source](https://www.schools.pima.gov/media/press-releases/educators-from-southern-arizona-selected-for-the-stemazing-teacher-leader-p)). She holds significant community standing as the 2018 Cochise County Teacher of the Year and a 2019 Arizona Teacher of the Year Ambassador for Excellence ([source](https://cochiseeducationfoundation.org/recognition/)) ([source](https://events.nau.edu/event/tariat/)). Darrel Howell is a federal employee with the USDA Forest Service, serving as a fire and fuels management contact for the Nogales Ranger District of the Coronado National Forest, which manages lands across Cochise, Pima, and Santa Cruz counties ([source](https://www.fs.usda.gov/sopa/components/reports/sopa-110305-2024-01.pdf)). Lee Ann Howell is documented attending meetings for the Sulphur Springs Valley Electric Cooperative (SSVEC) in Willcox, indicating engagement with local utility matters ([source](https://www.ssvec.org/downloads/minutes/Minutes-2016-07.pdf)).
neighbors.influence_justification: Award-winning educator and USDA Forest Service official

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage regarding environmental stewardship given Darrel's Forest Service role. Highlight tax revenue benefits for local schools, appealing to Lee Ann's position as a celebrated educator.
